nutritionPolicy: Compute nutrition at import time from the weighed ingredients using the nutrition engine; do not publish hand-entered estimates.
recipeProvenance: Original Sloe Kitchen wording developed from the canonical technique named on each recipe and written to match a founder-approved photograph.

recipes.title: Butter Bean Shakshuka
recipes.shortDescription: Soft eggs baked in a smoky tomato and red pepper sauce with creamy butter beans and feta.
recipes.culinaryBasis: Shakshuka with eggs poached in a pepper and tomato sauce
steps: Heat the olive oil in a 24cm lidded frying pan over medium heat. Add the onion and pepper and cook for 8–10 minutes, stirring often, until soft and beginning to colour.
Add the garlic, cumin, paprika and chilli flakes. Stir for 1 minute, just until fragrant.
Pour in the chopped tomatoes, add the salt and simmer uncovered for 10–12 minutes, until the sauce is thick enough for a spoon to leave a trail.
Fold in the butter beans and simmer for 3 minutes. Make three wells in the sauce and crack an egg into each one.
Cover and cook over low-medium heat for 5–7 minutes, until the whites are fully set but the yolks remain soft. Give the pan an extra minute if any white around the yolks is still translucent.
Scatter over the feta and parsley and serve from the pan.

recipes.title: Halloumi, Lentil & Roast Veg Salad
recipes.shortDescription: Charred halloumi, warm lentils and sweet roasted vegetables with a sharp lemon dressing.
recipes.culinaryBasis: Warm lentil salad with grilled halloumi and roasted vegetables

nutritionOverride.description: Squash, summer, zucchini, includes skin, raw

steps: Heat the oven to 220°C/200°C fan. Toss the courgette and pepper with half the olive oil, half the salt and the pepper on a large tray.
Roast for 20–25 minutes, turning once, until tender with browned edges rather than soft and watery.
Whisk the remaining olive oil with the lemon zest, 2 tablespoons of lemon juice, honey and remaining salt.
Warm the lentils in a small pan with 1 tablespoon of water, then take off the heat and toss with half the dressing and the parsley.
Heat a dry non-stick frying pan over medium-high heat. Cook the halloumi for about 2 minutes on each side, until deeply golden but still yielding in the middle.
Spread the lentils over a platter, add the roast vegetables and rocket, then top with halloumi and spoon over the remaining dressing.

recipes.title: Harissa Salmon with Herb Couscous
recipes.shortDescription: Sticky harissa-roasted salmon over lemony giant couscous, cucumber and fresh herbs.
recipes.culinaryBasis: Harissa-glazed roast salmon with lemony couscous
steps: Heat the oven to 210°C/190°C fan and line a small tray. Mix the harissa, honey and 1 teaspoon of the olive oil.
Pat the salmon dry, season with half the salt and brush the harissa mixture over the top and sides. Roast for 10–12 minutes, until opaque at the edges and just flaking in the centre.
Meanwhile, boil the giant couscous in well-salted water according to the packet, usually 8–10 minutes. Drain well and let it steam-dry for 1 minute.
Toss the warm couscous with the remaining olive oil, lemon zest, cucumber, parsley, mint, remaining salt and black pepper.
Divide the couscous between plates, set a salmon fillet on each and serve with lemon wedges.

recipes.title: Miso Salmon Rice Bowl
recipes.shortDescription: Caramelised miso salmon with rice, edamame, cool cucumber and pickled ginger.
steps: Rinse the rice until the water is nearly clear. Cook with 225ml water using the packet method, then rest off the heat, covered, for 10 minutes.
Heat the oven to 210°C/190°C fan. Mix the miso, soy, honey, 1 tablespoon of the rice vinegar and the sesame oil.
Put the salmon on a lined tray and spoon over the glaze. Roast for 10–12 minutes, until the salmon flakes at the thickest point and the glaze is caramelised but not burnt.
Toss the cucumber with the remaining rice vinegar. Leave for 10 minutes, then drain. Cook the edamame in boiling water for 3 minutes and drain.
Fluff the rice into two bowls. Add the cucumber, edamame and pickled ginger, top with salmon and finish with sesame seeds.

recipes.title: Tomato Butter Bean Orzo
recipes.shortDescription: A glossy one-pan tomato orzo with creamy butter beans, burst tomatoes and spinach.
recipes.culinaryBasis: One-pan tomato orzo with creamy white beans

nutritionOverride.description: Soup, vegetable broth, ready to serve

steps: Heat the oil in a wide sauté pan over medium heat. Cook the onion for 6 minutes, until soft, then add the garlic and chilli flakes for 30 seconds.
Add the cherry tomatoes and cook for 4–5 minutes, pressing only the softest ones so some burst while others stay whole.
Stir in the orzo, passata, stock and salt. Bring to a gentle simmer and cook uncovered for 10–12 minutes, stirring often so the pasta does not catch.
When the orzo is almost tender and the sauce is glossy, fold in the butter beans and spinach. Cook for 2–3 minutes, until the beans are hot and the spinach has wilted.
Take off the heat, fold through most of the basil and black pepper, and rest for 2 minutes. Spoon into bowls and finish with the remaining basil.

recipes.title: Prawn Saganaki with Feta
recipes.shortDescription: King prawns baked in a rich oregano tomato sauce with soft feta and crisp sourdough.
recipes.culinaryBasis: Greek prawn saganaki baked in tomato sauce with feta
steps: Heat the oven to 220°C/200°C fan. Pat the prawns dry and keep chilled while you make the sauce.
Heat 1 tablespoon of the oil in a 24cm ovenproof frying pan. Cook the onion with the salt for 7–8 minutes, until soft and lightly golden.
Add the garlic, chilli flakes and oregano for 1 minute. Add the tomatoes and simmer for 10 minutes, until rich and no longer watery.
Nestle the prawns into the sauce in a single layer and tuck the feta between them. Bake for 7–9 minutes, until every prawn is pink, opaque and curled into a loose C shape.
Brush the bread with the remaining oil and toast while the prawns bake. Finish the saganaki with parsley and black pepper and serve with the toast.

recipes.title: Green Herb Falafel Bowl
recipes.shortDescription: Crisp hand-shaped falafel with a green centre, herby bulgur, chopped salad and lemon tahini.
recipes.culinaryBasis: Chickpea falafel with tabbouleh-style grains and tahini
recipes.advanceNote: Soak the dried chickpeas in plenty of cold water for 12–18 hours; soaking time is not included above.

ingredients.preparation: soaked 12–18 hours and drained; do not cook

steps: For the falafel, pulse the drained chickpeas, onion, garlic, parsley, coriander, cumin, ground coriander and salt in a food processor. Stop while the mixture still resembles fine couscous; do not blend it into paste.
Mix in the baking powder and chickpea flour. Chill for 20 minutes, then squeeze a spoonful: it should hold together. Add 1 extra tablespoon of chickpea flour only if it crumbles.

Meanwhile, toss the red onion with the red wine vinegar and sugar and leave for at least 20 minutes. Cover the bulgur with 360ml boiling water, cover and leave for 15 minutes, then drain, fluff and mix with the chopped parsley.
Whisk the tahini and lemon juice—it will tighten—then add the cold water a little at a time until smooth and spoonable. Mix the tomato and cucumber separately.

Heat the oven to 220°C/200°C fan. Shape the falafel mixture into 16 compact ovals, arrange on a lined tray and brush all over with the rapeseed oil. Bake for 20–24 minutes, turning halfway, until deeply golden and firm at the centre.
Divide the bulgur and chopped salad between bowls. Drain the pickled onion, add it with four falafel to each bowl, and spoon over the lemon tahini.

recipes.title: Crispy Tofu Peanut Noodles
recipes.shortDescription: Golden tofu and rice noodles in a savoury peanut-lime sauce with crisp cucumber and carrot.
recipes.culinaryBasis: Rice noodles in peanut-lime sauce with crisp tofu

nutritionOverride.description: Tofu, extra firm, prepared with nigari

steps: Pat the tofu dry, toss with the cornflour and shake off any excess. Heat the oil in a wide non-stick pan over medium-high heat.
Cook the tofu for 8–10 minutes, turning every 2 minutes, until several sides are evenly golden and crisp. Transfer to a plate.
Cook or soak the rice noodles according to the packet. Drain, rinse briefly under cool water and drain very thoroughly.
Whisk the peanut butter, soy, lime juice and maple syrup, then add enough warm water to make a satin sauce that coats a spoon.
Toss the noodles with the peanut sauce, cucumber and carrot. Divide between bowls and top with the tofu, peanuts and coriander.

recipes.title: Ginger Chicken Udon
recipes.shortDescription: Glossy ginger-soy udon with browned chicken, pak choi and carrot ribbons.
recipes.culinaryBasis: Stir-fried udon with ginger, soy, chicken and greens
steps: Loosen the udon under warm running water and drain. Mix the soy, vinegar, honey, sesame oil and 2 tablespoons of water.

Heat half the rapeseed oil in a wok or wide frying pan over high heat. Cook the chicken in a single layer for 5–6 minutes, turning once, until browned and fully opaque through the thickest piece. Transfer to a plate.
Add the remaining oil and place the pak choi cut-side down. Cook for 2 minutes, then add the carrot, ginger and garlic and stir-fry for 1 minute.
Return the chicken, add the udon and pour in the sauce. Toss over high heat for 2–3 minutes, until the noodles are hot, glossy and separated.
Divide between bowls and finish with spring onion and sesame seeds.

recipes.title: Gochujang Turkey Meatballs with Sesame Rice
recipes.shortDescription: Hand-rolled turkey meatballs in a sticky gochujang glaze with rice and broccoli.
recipes.culinaryBasis: Turkey meatballs glazed with gochujang, soy and honey
steps: Heat the oven to 220°C/200°C fan. Mix the turkey, panko, egg, spring onion, garlic, ginger and salt just until combined; overmixing makes the meatballs firm.
With damp hands, roll 18 meatballs, deliberately leaving them slightly irregular rather than compacting them into perfect spheres. Set on a lined tray.
Bake for 14–17 minutes, until lightly browned and 74°C in the centre. Meanwhile, cook the rice and steam the broccoli until tender with a little bite.
Whisk the gochujang, soy, honey, vinegar, sesame oil and 3 tablespoons of water in a wide frying pan. Simmer for 2–3 minutes until glossy.
Add the cooked meatballs and turn over low heat for 2 minutes, until evenly coated and piping hot.
Divide the rice and broccoli between bowls, add six meatballs to each and finish with sesame seeds.

recipes.title: Thai Basil Beef with Rice
recipes.shortDescription: Fast, glossy chilli-basil beef with jasmine rice, cucumber and a crisp-edged fried egg.
recipes.culinaryBasis: Pad kra pao-style minced beef with basil, chilli and fried egg
steps: Cook the rice according to the packet and keep covered. Mix the oyster sauce, fish sauce, soy, sugar and 2 tablespoons of water.
Heat half the oil in a wok or large frying pan over high heat. Fry the eggs one at a time until the whites are fully set with crisp edges and the yolks remain soft. Set aside.
Add the remaining oil, then the beef. Spread it out and leave for 1 minute before stir-frying for 3–4 minutes, until browned with no pink mince remaining.
Add the shallot, garlic and chilli and stir-fry for 90 seconds. Pour in the sauce and toss for 1–2 minutes, until it clings to the beef.
Take the pan off the heat and fold in the Thai basil just until wilted. Serve with the rice, fried eggs and cucumber.

recipes.title: Sesame Soba & Edamame Salad
recipes.shortDescription: Cool buckwheat noodles, edamame and crunchy vegetables in a bright sesame-soy dressing.
recipes.culinaryBasis: Cold soba noodle salad with sesame-soy dressing
steps: Whisk the soy, rice vinegar, sesame oil, maple syrup and ginger in a large mixing bowl.
Cook the soba in a large pan of boiling water according to the packet, usually 4–6 minutes. Add the edamame for the final 3 minutes.
Drain immediately and rinse under cold running water, gently lifting the noodles with your fingers until completely cool. Drain very thoroughly.
Add the noodles and edamame to the dressing with the cabbage, cucumber and most of the spring onion and sesame seeds. Toss gently until evenly coated.
Divide between bowls and finish with the remaining spring onion and sesame seeds.

recipes.title: Coconut Lime Chicken Curry
recipes.shortDescription: Tender chicken and green vegetables in a silky red-curry coconut sauce sharpened with lime.
recipes.culinaryBasis: Thai-inspired coconut chicken curry with lime and green vegetables

ingredients.preparation: one juiced, one cut into wedges

steps: Cook the jasmine rice according to the packet and keep covered while the curry cooks.
Heat the oil in a wide saucepan over medium heat. Fry the curry paste for 90 seconds, stirring, until fragrant and the oil turns red.
Add the chicken and stir for 3–4 minutes, until every piece is coated and the outside has lost its raw colour.
Pour in the coconut milk and stock. Bring to a gentle simmer and cook uncovered for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the green beans and pepper and simmer for 5–7 minutes more, until the vegetables are tender-crisp and the chicken reaches 75°C at the centre.
Stir in the fish sauce, sugar and lime juice. Taste for a balance of salt, sweetness and acidity, then serve with rice, coriander and lime wedges.

recipes.title: Soy Ginger Cod with Bok Choy
recipes.shortDescription: Steam-roasted cod with a light soy-ginger glaze, jasmine rice and glossy bok choy.
recipes.culinaryBasis: Steam-roasted cod with soy, ginger and greens
steps: Heat the oven to 200°C/180°C fan. Cook the jasmine rice according to the packet and keep covered.
Mix the soy, ginger, garlic, honey, rice vinegar, sesame oil and 2 tablespoons of water.
Set the cod in a snug baking dish and spoon over half the sauce. Cover tightly with foil and bake for 10–13 minutes, until opaque and flaking at the thickest point, or 63°C in the centre.
Meanwhile, heat the rapeseed oil in a wide frying pan. Put the bok choy cut-side down and cook for 2 minutes, add 2 tablespoons of water, cover and steam for 2 minutes more.
Divide the rice and bok choy between plates. Add the cod, spoon over the remaining sauce and finish with spring onions and sesame seeds.

recipes.title: Kimchi Fried Rice with Eggs
recipes.shortDescription: Savoury kimchi fried rice with two crisp-edged eggs, spring onion, sesame and nori.

nutritionOverride.description: Rice, white, long-grain, cooked without salt

steps: Mix the gochujang, soy, sesame oil and reserved kimchi juice. Break up any clumps in the cold rice with your fingers.
Heat 1 tablespoon of rapeseed oil in a large frying pan or wok over high heat. Add the kimchi and stir-fry for 2–3 minutes, until its liquid has mostly cooked away.
Add the rice and spread it across the pan. Leave for 1 minute, then stir-fry for 3–4 minutes, until every grain is coated and the rice is piping hot throughout.
Pour in the gochujang mixture and toss for 1 minute. Fold through half the spring onion and keep warm over low heat.
Heat the remaining oil in a separate pan and fry the eggs until the whites are fully set with crisp edges and soft yolks.
Spoon the rice into a warm skillet or bowls, top with both eggs and finish with nori, sesame and the remaining spring onion.
recipes.safetyNote: Use rice that was cooled quickly after cooking and kept refrigerated; reheat only once and make sure it is piping hot throughout.

recipes.title: Mango Prawn Rice Bowl
recipes.shortDescription: Chilli-lime king prawns with fragrant rice, ripe mango and a crisp cabbage-cucumber salad.
recipes.culinaryBasis: Chilli-lime prawns with mango, rice and crunchy vegetables
steps: Cook the jasmine rice according to the packet, then rest covered for 10 minutes and fluff with a fork.
Toss the cucumber and cabbage with half the lime juice, the honey and half the salt. Leave to soften slightly while you cook the prawns.
Mix the prawns with the lime zest, chilli, garlic, remaining salt and rapeseed oil. Do not leave them in the lime mixture for more than 10 minutes before cooking.
Heat a large frying pan over high heat. Add the prawns in one layer and cook for 90 seconds per side, until pink, opaque and curled into loose C shapes.
Divide the rice between bowls. Add the dressed cabbage and cucumber, mango and prawns, then finish with the remaining lime juice and coriander.

recipes.title: Huevos Rancheros with Black Beans
recipes.shortDescription: Crisp-edged eggs and warm tomato-chilli salsa over toasted corn tortillas, with black beans and avocado.
recipes.culinaryBasis: Fried eggs, tomato-chilli salsa and black beans over corn tortillas
steps: Heat 1 teaspoon of the oil in a small saucepan over medium heat. Add half the onion and cook for 4 minutes, then add the garlic, jalapeño and half the cumin for 30 seconds.
Add the chopped tomatoes and half the salt. Simmer for 8–10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the salsa is thick and spoonable but still visibly chunky.
Put the beans, remaining onion, remaining cumin and 3 tablespoons of water in another small pan. Simmer for 5 minutes, crushing only a few beans so the mixture stays mostly whole.
Warm a dry frying pan over high heat. Toast the tortillas for about 30 seconds per side, until hot with a few dark spots, then place one on each plate.
Add the remaining oil to the pan and fry the eggs until the whites are fully set with crisp edges and the yolks remain soft.
Set an egg on each tortilla and spoon over the salsa. Serve with the black beans, avocado, lime wedges and coriander.

recipes.title: Chicken Tinga Rice Bowl
recipes.shortDescription: Juicy chipotle-tomato shredded chicken with brown rice, black beans, charred corn and avocado.

nutritionOverride.description: La Costeña Chipotle Peppers in Adobo Sauce

steps: Heat the oil in a wide lidded sauté pan over medium heat. Cook the onion with half the salt for 8–10 minutes, until very soft and lightly golden.
Add the garlic, chipotle, oregano and cumin and stir for 1 minute. Add the tomatoes and 150ml water and bring to a simmer.
Nestle the chicken breasts into the sauce, cover and simmer gently for 16–20 minutes, turning once, until the thickest piece reaches 75°C.
Lift the chicken onto a board and shred it with two forks. Simmer the uncovered sauce for 5 minutes, then return the chicken and cook for 2 minutes so the strands absorb the sauce.

Meanwhile, toss the sliced red onion with the red wine vinegar and sugar and leave to pickle for at least 20 minutes. Cook the brown rice according to the packet. Warm the black beans with 3 tablespoons of water and the remaining salt.
Heat a dry frying pan over high heat and cook the sweetcorn for 4–5 minutes, stirring only occasionally, until patched with dark char.
Divide the rice between four bowls. Add black beans, charred corn and avocado, spoon the chicken tinga across the centre and finish with the drained pickled red onion.
